_confidence_levels.firered-anchor: Vanilla FireRed address from the pokefirered decomp / community RAM maps. NOT confirmed for Unbound — verify against a live read before trusting.
_confidence_levels.unbound-verified: Confirmed against Unbound specifically (community GameShark code, or a live diff-scanner result that unambiguously matches a known value).
_confidence_levels.candidate: Found via diff-scanner from ONE observed action. Plausible but not cross-verified — could be coincidental or part of an unrelated adjacent field. Needs a second, differently-shaped test before trusting.

pointers.name: gSaveBlock1Ptr
pointers.note: LE u32 pointing into WRAM; money, player position, flags, vars live inside this struct

party.name: gPlayerParty
party.note2: Confirmed on real hardware 2026-07-22: decoded Lv/HP/stats matched the actual in-game party exactly.
party.note3: Species/nickname decode (Gen3Decrypt) confirmed correct 2026-07-22 across all 6 party slots + full enemy team, despite the struct's built-in checksum failing every time. Layout/decryption is right; Unbound most likely changed the checksum FORMULA (e.g. folding in extra data for its custom Mega Evolution/mission systems) rather than the substructure layout. Treat checksum as informational, not a trust gate.
party.note4: Moves/PP came back nonsensical (PP values above the real max) despite correct species -- the hand-transcribed substructure-order table had several wrong rows (Growth's position happened to still be right on those rows, which is why species decoded fine while Attacks/EVs/Misc were scrambled). Replaced 2026-07-22 with the verified table from PKHeX's PokeCrypto.cs (kwsch/PKHeX, an actively-maintained open-source Gen3+ save editor).
party.note5: PP still wrong after the table swap -- root cause was actually the DIRECTION the table was applied, not the values. Confirmed against PKHeX's Decrypt3 call site: canonical[i] = raw[order[i]], a direct lookup. The code was inverting it (searching for where each identity appeared instead of indexing directly), which only produced correct results on rows whose 3-element sub-permutation was a self-inverse swap -- matching the exact symptom of species always right, moves right on some slots and not others. Fixed 2026-07-22.
party.note: 6 x 100-byte Gen3 mon structs. Level/HP/stats at offsets 0x54-0x63 are UNENCRYPTED and directly readable for verification.

enemyParty.name: gEnemyParty
enemyParty.note: Battle only. Confirmed 2026-07-22: decoded species/nicknames matched the real enemy team.

overworldObjects.name: gObjectEvents
overworldObjects.note: Slot 0 is the player. Community FireRed RAM map, cross-referenced 2026-07-22. Sub-field offsets (x/y coords, facing direction) are NOT mapped yet -- struct is opaque for now. Use the diff scanner while walking to locate the x/y bytes within this 36-byte block.

coordAxisA_low.note: 0x02036E48 (obj+0x10). Changed 0x1F->0x1E (-1) on a single step, 2026-07-22. Likely the low byte of a u16 coordinate (high byte didn't change, consistent with a small position value under 256) -- one of currentCoords.x/y or a related field.

coordAxisA_low_2.note: 0x02036E4C (obj+0x14), exactly 4 bytes after coordAxisA_low. Changed identically (0x1F->0x1E) on the SAME step (confirmed 2026-07-22 this was a pure X-axis step) -- both fields moving together on a single-axis step supports current+previous copies of X, not X and Y. Still need a pure Y-axis (north/south) step to find the Y offset pair and fully confirm.

scriptVars.name: gSpecialVars (0x8000-0x800F)
scriptVars.note: Engine-level scratch variables used by map scripts. Likely where Unbound's custom mission/difficulty/story flags live, given it heavily extends the script system. Values are transient/context-dependent, not fixed per-var meanings.

anchors.name: playerName
anchors.note: Read as all zeros on real Unbound hardware 2026-07-22 — likely shifted, needs re-discovery via diff scanner.

anchors.name: rivalName
anchors.note: Confirmed 2026-07-22: decodes to "Ryan", matches the player's real rival name.

anchors.name: currentMapBank
anchors.note: Confirmed 2026-07-22: changed consistently and distinctly moving between buildings and routes. Live current map, not just the entry-warp snapshot like prevMapBank.

anchors.name: cheatItemSlot
anchors.note: Target of Unbound item GameShark codes (e.g. 0x028A=Psychic Memory, 0x0250=Flying Gem). Likely a give-item/bag-add slot.

anchors.name: repelStepCount
anchors.note: Confirmed 2026-07-22: using a Max Repel changed 0x00 -> 0xFA. 0xFA = 250 decimal, exactly the step count a Max Repel grants — strong, non-coincidental match. Try a regular Repel (should write 100/0x64) or Super Repel (200/0xC8) to double-confirm, and watch it count down while walking.

anchors.name: battleStateFlag
anchors.note: Changed 0x00 -> 0x40 on entering a wild battle (before attacking), diff-scanner result from a single test. Hypothesis: a battle type/state flag byte. To confirm: check the value across a trainer battle, a double battle, and after the battle ends (should return to 0x00) - if it's consistently the same shape per battle type, it's real.

anchors.name: fieldCounterNearRepel
anchors.note: Changed 0x04 -> 0x00 on entering a wild battle, diff-scanner result from a single test. Sits exactly 4 bytes after repelStepCount, so it may be an adjacent field in the same small struct rather than something battle-related. To confirm: watch it while walking with no repel active (does it count steps or encounters?), and see if it resets on other triggers besides battle entry.

anchors.name: stringBuffer0
anchors.note: Community FireRed RAM map: general-purpose decoded string buffer, used for various UI text. Candidate for the 'Route 9'/'Tehl Town' location banner text -- walk into a new map and read this right after the banner shows to test.

anchors.name: stringBuffer1
anchors.note: Community FireRed RAM map: general-purpose decoded string buffer. Same location-banner test as stringBuffer0.

anchors.name: stringBuffer2
anchors.note: Community FireRed RAM map: general-purpose decoded string buffer. Same location-banner test as stringBuffer0.

anchors.name: msgBoxString
anchors.note: Community FireRed RAM map: 'string to be displayed in a message box' -- size is a guess (32B), the real field may be longer or shorter. Same location-banner test as stringBuffer0.
